memoryArtifacts.headline: The Frank Family Tree by the Thoresby Society
memoryArtifacts.description: This family tree is based on records held by the West Yorkshire Archive Service at Bradford (SpSt/6/3/15 and SpSt/1/3)--The Thoresby Society of Leeds and District.

zh.of Harewood, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾希亞活
zh.Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾阿爾武德利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Frank於大約1326年出生在英國越可爾阿爾武德利時，她的父親Nicholas Franke42歲，母親Ellen Ellis29歲。她於大約1343年與William Gascoigne Sr.在英國越可爾阿爾武德利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.of Harewood, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾希亞活
zh-hans.Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾阿爾武德利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Frank于大约1326年出生在英国越可爾阿爾武德利时，她的父亲Nicholas Franke42岁，母亲Ellen Ellis29岁。她于大约1343年与William Gascoigne Sr.在英国越可爾阿爾武德利结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Frank
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, Dutch, Scandinavian, Slovenian, Croatian, Czech, Slovak, Polish, Hungarian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): ethnic or habitational name for someone from Franconia (German Franken), a region of southwestern Germany so called from its early settlement by the Franks, an ancient Germanic people who inhabited the lands around the River Rhine in Roman times. In the 6th–9th centuries, under leaders such as Clovis I (c. 466–511) and Charlemagne (742–814), the Franks established a substantial empire in western Europe, from which the country of France takes its name.

English (of Norman origin), Dutch, and German: from the personal name Frank (Norman French Franco, ancient Germanic Franko), in origin an ethnic name for a Frank, or from German Franke ‘Frank(ish), Franconian’ (compare 1 above). This also came to be used as an adjective meaning ‘free, open-hearted, generous’ (Middle English and Old French franc ‘free’, i.e. not a serf or slave), deriving from the fact that in Frankish Gaul only people of Frankish race enjoyed the status of fully free men. As a surname of German origin it is also found (in both possible meanings; see 1 above) in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Franc and Franck .

variants.name: Franco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Ruben, Alfredo, Miguel, Ricardo, Jorge.  Portuguese Paulo, Joao, Godofredo, Nelio.  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Marco, Angelo, Carmine, Lorenzo, Carlo, Elio, Enrico, Mauro, Rocco, Dante.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Franco, in origin an ethnic name for a Frank (see Frank 2).
Italian and Spanish: nickname or status name from franco ‘free’ (usually denoting a freed slave).

variants.name: Francke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Lothar, Bernhard, Hans, Markus, Mathias.
Dutch, Danish, German, and English: variant of   Frank  .

variants.name: Franke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Fritz, Erwin, Otto, Armin, Wolfgang, Ernst, Guenter, Horst, Klaus, Siegfried.
German, Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Franco (‘the Frank’); see Frank 1 and 2.
English: variant of   Frank  .

variants.name: Franc
meanings: French and Catalan: from the ancient Germanic personal name Franko, in origin an ethnic name for a Frank, which also came to be used as an adjective meaning ‘free, open-hearted, generous’ (Old French franc ‘free’, i.e. not a serf or slave); see Frank 2. Compare French Franck 2.
Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from a Slavicized form of the German personal name   Franz  .

Slovenian and Croatian: from a vernacular form of the personal names Slovenian Frančišek, Croatian Francisko (from Latin Franciscus; see Francis ), derived from or influenced by German Franz . Compare Frantz 2 and Franck 4.

variants.name: Frangos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Costas, Koula, Christos, Demetrios, Despina, Dimitrios, Ilias, Pantelis, Speros, Spiros, Spyridon, Stamatios.
Greek:
from Frankos, literally ‘French’ or ‘Frankish’ (see Frank ), but applied by extension to all Western Europeans and all Catholics (including ethnic Greek Catholics).

variants.name: Franck
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Camille, Edouard, Francois, Pierre, Yanick.
German, Danish, and Dutch: variant of   Frank  .
French: variant of   Frank   2 and   Franc   1.

variants.name: Franks
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from   Frank  .
English: perhaps sometimes a variant of Frankish, an ethnic name from Middle English frankys ‘French, Frenchman’ (Old English frencisc), for someone from France.

variants.name: Franken
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Gerhard, Klaus.
Dutch: patronymic from   Frank  .
German: patronymic from   Franke  .

variants.name: Frankl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Erwin, Gerda, Gitta, Gunther, Rainer.  Jewish Chaim, Chaskel, Meyer.
German (rarely Fränkl) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of Frank .

variants.name: Frankart
meanings: German: probably a habitational name from a place called with Frank + Middle High German hard ‘woods for grazing’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Frank was born about 1326, in Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Nicholas Franke, was 42 and her mother, Ellen Ellis, was 29. She married William Gascoigne Sr. about 1343, in Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Frank est née environ 1326, à Alwoodley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Franke, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Ellen Ellis, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé William Gascoigne Sr. environ 1343, à Alwoodley, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Frank um 1326 in Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Franke 42 und ihre Mutter Ellen Ellis 29. Sie hat um 1343 in Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England William Gascoigne Sr. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Frank è nata il circa 1326 nel Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Nicholas Franke, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Ellen Ellis, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato William Gascoigne Sr. circa 1343, nel Alwoodley, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Frankが約1326年にイギリスヨークシュアイアアルウッドリーで生まれた時，父Nicholas Frankeは42歳，母Ellen Ellisは29歳であった。約1343年にイギリスヨークシュアイアアルウッドリーでWilliam Gascoigne Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Frank(이)가 대략 1326년 영국 요르크실르 알우들리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Franke(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen Ellis(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 대략 1343년 영국 요르크실르 알우들리에서 William Gascoigne Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

mn.of Harewood, Yorkshire, England: Harewood, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Frank nasceu aproximadamente 1326, em Alwoodley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Franke, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Ellen Ellis, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com William Gascoigne Sr. aproximadamente 1343, em Alwoodley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Frank родилась около 1326 г. в местности алвудли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Franke было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Ellen Ellis, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Gascoigne Sr. около 1343 г. в местности алвудли, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Frank nació aproximadamente 1326, en Alwoodley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Franke, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Ellen Ellis, tenía 29 años. Se casó con William Gascoigne Sr. aproximadamente 1343, en Alwoodley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas.
